steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank joins two independent clauses: 'The book wasn't written solely by Legler' and 'other authors, such as Stephen Graham Jones and Barry Lopez, also contributed essays,' with 'however' marking the contrast. The correct choice must punctuate the independent-clause boundary and set off 'however' correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Legler, however,: Comma before and comma after 'however' joins two independent clauses with only commas — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — Legler; however: Missing the comma after "however" at the start of the second independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — Legler. However: Missing the comma after "However" at the start of the second sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — Legler, however;: The comma binds 'however' to IC1 as a trailing parenthetical, qualifying the claim that the book was written solely by Legler. The semicolon then joins IC2 about the other contributors. Parse: [IC1 + trailing 'however']; [IC2]. The contrast attaches directly to the sole-authorship assertion — the most natural semantic fit. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
